Patch Set 10:

(1 comment)

According to John Hammond:
> this patch keeps adding to the service time estimate.
> With 7 clients, I ran replay-single.sh 0d a few times. It took 93s, 183s, 302s, 423s, ... 608s.
> It does solve LU-5077.

Patch Set 10:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.5/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/bf8b1b30-58fd-11e4-8f95-5254006e85c2).  Ran 16 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 10:

>It took 93s, 183s, 302s, 423s, ... 608s.
Yeap, AT would be increased. You could see failed test also, if AT would be long enough to miss request resend into recovery window. But this relates for tests only. And this is the best solution for the early reply issue, from my point of view.
I have found that AT feature is bad during recovery and makes worse prediction for later Lustre work. During recovery server is not high loaded and clients send requests one by one, so it is not right to base recovery on those AT estimations.  The best way to improve this situation - is remove AT from recovery phase.
Patch Set 10: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 1d889090f2e2902d861d1fab0227c4343127cc42

parents.subject: LU-5752 doc: Added missing manpages to Makefile.am

commit.subject: LU-5079 ptlrpc: fix early reply timeout for recovery
commit.message:
LU-5079 ptlrpc: fix early reply timeout for recovery

Commit 8b2f9c0e408 http://review.whamcloud.com/9100 changed
the deadline calculation from current time to request arrival.
During recovery, the new deadline could be less than calculated
at request arrival time. And even worse, the deadline may be
in past. For the first case unneeded early reply would be sent
to client. For the second, client requests would be dropped by
timeout and client reconnect happend.

Do at_measured() for recovery in the same way like general early
reply base on the current time. And set new timeout to the end
of recovery.

Test to check recovery deadline bug.

Signed-off-by: Alexander Boyko <alexander_boyko@xyratex.com>
Change-Id: I29327cb962d1c1a3cd8a6181d27a29593d1d8fc4
Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-1988
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/11213
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Niu Yawei <yawei.niu@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Chris Horn <hornc@cray.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
